items.question: Sequential await vs Promise.all — when does it matter?
items.answer: When calls are independent. Awaiting them one by one runs them in series (sum of latencies); Promise.all starts them together and waits once (roughly the slowest call). For dependent calls, sequential is required.

items.question: Why doesn't await freeze the whole server?
items.answer: Because JavaScript's event loop is non-blocking: await suspends only the current async function and frees the loop to handle other requests until the awaited Promise settles.

items.question: What breaks if you forget to await an async model call?
items.answer: Execution continues before the result exists, so you may use undefined data, create race conditions, and miss errors that surface as unhandled rejections.
